source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: Conventional IVM systems face fundamental trade-offs between resolution, FOV, DOF, and system form factor
PrecisionView achieves significant improvement in both FOV and DOF through a simple optical layout and compact design
The extended FOV and DOF simplify large-area image mosaicking during manual scanning
PrecisionView generates large-scale coregistered maps of cellular and vascular structures
Results suggest the potential to address the unmet need for early cancer detection at the point of care

typical_pattern: Contrast with prior approaches or conventional systems
Explain technical mechanism or design principle
Discuss implications for clinical translation or future work

likely_priorities: Validation of resolution claims across the full FOV and DOF
Comparison with existing IVM systems in clinical settings
Demonstration of diagnostic accuracy in precancer detection
Robustness of reconstruction in heterogeneous tissue with scattering
Clinical workflow integration and real-time performance
likely_attacks: Peak in-focus contrast may be modestly lower than conventional diffraction-limited systems
Motion blur visible in videos when probe is translated across tissue
System designed primarily for superficial epithelial imaging where scattering remains moderate
Effective acquisition frame rate limited by long exposure time required for fluorescence imaging
Insufficient validation in deeper or highly heterogeneous tissue with scattering-induced aberrations
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The discussion explicitly acknowledges that peak in-focus contrast at the exact focal plane may be modestly lower than conventional systems, reflecting the trade-off of wavefront coding approaches. The authors note that motion blur is visible in videos during probe translation and that the system is designed primarily for superficial epithelial imaging where scattering-induced aberrations remain moderate. They discuss that in deeper or highly heterogeneous tissue, scattering can introduce additional distortions, and future work could improve robustness by incorporating simulated or experimentally measured scattering conditions. The effective acquisition frame rate limitation due to long exposure time is also discussed as a constraint. These self-identified limitations form the basis for inferring likely reviewer concerns about validation depth, clinical robustness, and performance trade-offs.

examples.quoted_phrase: Our results suggest the potential of this computational endomicroscope to address the unmet need for early cancer detection at the point of care
PrecisionView achieves significant improvement in both FOV and DOF through a simple optical layout and compact design, which are otherwise unachievable with conventional optics
The extended FOV demonstrated by PrecisionView reflects an increase in the effective usable field rather than simply an expansion of the geometric field defined by the optics and sensor
Although PrecisionView is designed to provide extended DOF, its peak in-focus contrast at the exact focal plane may be modestly lower than that of a conventional diffraction-limited system
These developments highlight how optical–computational imaging approaches can be translated from proof-of-concept demonstrations to clinically relevant imaging systems

phrase_evidence.phrase: In this study, we present a compact, affordable ($3,000), AI-powered endomicroscope
The present work builds upon prior phase-mask–based extended DOF approaches while extending these concepts toward a clinically deployable in vivo imaging system
In contrast to earlier implementations
Importantly, the deployment of real-time deep learning reconstruction enables immediate all-in-focus visualization
These developments highlight how optical–computational imaging approaches can be translated from proof-of-concept demonstrations to clinically relevant imaging systems
Conventional IVM systems face fundamental trade-offs between resolution, FOV, DOF, and system form factor
However, these approaches increase system complexity and cost and are difficult to implement in handheld imaging systems due to their sensitivity to motion
In contrast, PrecisionView achieves significant improvement in both FOV and DOF through a simple optical layout and compact design
Although PrecisionView is designed to provide extended DOF, its peak in-focus contrast at the exact focal plane may be modestly lower
This behavior reflects the trade-off of wavefront coding approaches
There is a tradeoff between optical sharpness at the nominal focal plane and improved preservation of spatial frequency information across defocus
PrecisionView currently is designed primarily for superficial epithelial imaging where scattering-induced aberrations remain moderate
Future work could improve robustness by incorporating simulated or experimentally measured scattering conditions into the training process
The PrecisionView system can be readily assembled using off-the-shelf components and a customized phase mask at low cost
Unlike most computational microscopy techniques that rely on extensive postprocessing, PrecisionView integrates rapid deep learning reconstruction directly into the video acquisition pipeline
motion blur is visible in the videos when the probe is translated across tissue
To mitigate this effect, we implemented a handheld stop-and-go acquisition strategy
The real-time reconstruction speed is determined by the computation capability of the hardware
In future prototypes, we plan to integrate miniature LEDs at the distal end of the PrecisionView
Although image mosaicking has been used to expand the FOV of conventional IVM systems, the total stitched imaging area remains limited
In contrast, the FOV and extended DOF of PrecisionView simplify large-area image mosaicking during manual scanning
Our results suggest the potential of this computational endomicroscope to address the unmet need for early cancer detection at the point of care
representing approximately 5× increase in FOV and 8× larger DOF compared to conventional IVM with similar resolution
PrecisionView generates large-scale (1 to 3 cm2) coregistered maps of cellular and vascular structures
revealing distinct microscopic patterns associated with anatomic structures and precancerous lesions
PrecisionView is a compact, handheld, and simple-to-use endomicroscope designed for high-resolution, noninvasive
The device incorporates a simple conjugated microscope design with off-the-shelf lenses
Experimentally captured images of 4 μm diameter fluorescent beads using PrecisionView and the conventional system

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, invented numbers, or author names were introduced. Claim strength examples and phrase evidence are grounded in the provided discussion, abstract, and figure legends. Reviewer preference profile is inferred from explicit limitations and caveats stated by the authors.
